claimText: India's first Dugong Conservation Reserve was established in Palk Bay by the Tamil Nadu government.
evidenceExcerpt: India's first Dugong Conservation Reserve was established in Palk Bay by the Tamil Nadu government.
examUse: Statement-based question on protected areas and state initiatives.

claimText: The IUCN conservation status of the Dugong is Vulnerable (VU), and it is protected under Schedule I of the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972.
evidenceExcerpt: IUCN Status is Vulnerable (VU) and protected under WPA, 1972: Schedule I.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on species conservation status.

claimText: In India, the habitat of Dugongs includes Palk Bay, the Gulf of Mannar, and the Gulf of Kutch.
evidenceExcerpt: In India, they are found in Palk Bay, Gulf of Mannar, and Gulf of Kutch.
examUse: Statement-based question on the geographical distribution of marine species in India.

claimText: Dugongs are the only marine herbivorous mammals and they depend on seagrass for their diet.
evidenceExcerpt: They are the only marine herbivorous mammals that depend on seagrass (known as 'sea cows').
examUse: Statement-based question on species characteristics and ecological niche.
